provenance.source_corpus: Post-harvest Regeneration of Lowland Black Spruce Forests in Northeastern Ontario

source_spans.text: The CLAAG method resulted in highest total regeneration density of other conifers.
Density of other conifer was significantly lower on SHE sites compared to CLAAG and GST treatments.

nodes.name: group seed tree followed by shearblading site preparation (SHE)

source_spans.text: Density of other conifer was significantly lower on SHE sites compared to CLAAG and GST treatments.
Total black spruce regeneration density did not differ significantly among harvest methods, but increased significantly with time since harvest for CLAAG and GST harvest methods
Density of other conifers varied significantly among harvest methods and increased with time since harvest for CLAAG and GST methods
Stocking of black spruce, all conifers, or all tree species did not differ significantly among harvest methods, nor did it change with time since harvest.
Stocking of black spruce, all conifers, or all tree species did not differ significantly among harvest methods, nor did it change with time since harvest.
Stocking of black spruce, all conifers, or all tree species did not differ significantly among harvest methods, nor did it change with time since harvest.
Total black spruce regeneration density did not differ significantly among harvest methods, but increased significantly with time since harvest for CLAAG and GST harvest methods
Stocking was nonlinearly related to regeneration density.
